# `!proxmox_node`

**Category:** proxmox

Passive/read-only query of Proxmox node status (uptime, load, memory, CPU), optionally with cluster quorum and VM/LXC lists. Saves a JSON blob into a host variable.

## Parameters

| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `api_host` | string | yes | Proxmox API host. |
| `api_token_id` | string | yes | Proxmox API token ID. |
| `api_token_secret` | string | yes | Proxmox API token secret. |
| `node` | string | yes | Node name to query. |
| `save_to` | string | no | Variable name to store the resulting JSON info string into. |
| `include_cluster` | bool | no | Also fetch /cluster/status (adds cluster list + quorate bool). |
| `include_vms` | bool | no | Also list QEMU VMs on the node (adds vms + vm_count). |
| `include_lxc` | bool | no | Also list LXCs on the node (adds lxc + lxc_count). |

## Examples

```yaml
- !proxmox_node
  api_host: '{{ proxmox_host }}'
  api_token_id: '{{ proxmox_token_id }}'
  api_token_secret: '{{ proxmox_token_secret }}'
  node: bee
  save_to: node_info
  include_cluster: true
  include_vms: true
```
